 {"id":"bd-2q6d","title":"Beads commands operate on stale database without warning","description":"All beads read operations should validate database is in sync with JSONL before proceeding.

**Current Behavior:**
- Commands can query/read from stale database
- Only mutation operations (like 'bd sync') check if JSONL is newer
- User gets incorrect results without realizing database is out of sync

**Expected Behavior:**
- All beads commands should have pre-flight check for database freshness
- If JSONL is newer than database, refuse to operate with error: "Database out of sync. Run 'bd import' first."
- Same safety check that exists for 'bd sync' should apply to ALL operations

**Impact:**
- Users make decisions based on incomplete/outdated data
- Silent failures lead to confusion (e.g., thinking issues don't exist when they do)
- Similar to running git commands on stale repo without being warned to pull

**Example:**
- Searched for bd-g9eu issue file: not found
- Issue exists in .beads/issues.jsonl (in git)
- Database was stale, but no warning was given
- Led to incorrect conclusion that issue was already closed/deleted","notes":"## Implementation Complete

**Phase 1: Created staleness check (cmd/bd/staleness.go)**
- ensureDatabaseFresh() function checks JSONL mtime vs last_import_time
- Returns error with helpful message when database is stale
- Auto-skips in daemon mode (daemon has auto-import)

**Phase 2: Added to all read commands**
- list, show, ready, status, stale, info, duplicates, validate
- Check runs before database queries in direct mode
- Daemon mode already protected via checkAndAutoImportIfStale()

**Phase 3: Code Review Findings**
See follow-up issues:
- bd-XXXX: Add warning when staleness check errors
- bd-YYYY: Improve CheckStaleness error handling
- bd-ZZZZ: Refactor redundant daemon checks (low priority)

**Testing:**
- Build successful: go build ./cmd/bd
- Binary works: ./bd --version
- Ready for manual testing

**Next Steps:**
1. Test with stale database scenario
2. Implement review improvements
3. Close issue when tests pass","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T19:33:40.019297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"bd-fom","title":"Remove all deletions.jsonl code except migration","description":"There's deletions manifest code spread across the entire codebase that should have been removed after tombstone migration:

Files with deletions code (non-migration):
- internal/deletions/ - entire package
- cmd/bd/sync.go - 25+ references, auto-compact, sanitize
- cmd/bd/delete.go - dual-writes to deletions.jsonl
- internal/importer/importer.go - checks deletions manifest
- internal/syncbranch/worktree.go - merges deletions.jsonl
- cmd/bd/doctor/fix/sync.go - cleanupDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/doctor/fix/deletions.go - HydrateDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/integrity.go - checks deletions for data loss
- cmd/bd/deleted.go - entire command
- cmd/bd/compact.go - pruneDeletionsManifest
- cmd/bd/doctor.go - checkDeletionsManifest
- Plus many more

Action: Aggressively remove all non-migration deletions code. Tombstones are the only deletion mechanism now.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:29:04.960863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

- integrations/beads-mcp/src/beads_mcp/__init__.py

- Hook templates
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md (adds date)

 {"id":"bd-wisp-c3g","title":"mol-refinery-patrol","description":"Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:51:49.272262-08:00","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:51:49.272262-08:00","ephemeral":true}
